posts.text:
Other than that, Mrs. Lincoln, how was the play?


Ryan Petersen (@typesfast)

Guys, relax, the only risk in transiting the Strait of Hormuz is that Iran will shoot a missile at your ship. Otherwise it's open for transit. 

An all-time quote from our Secretary of War.

Video
  





— https://nitter.net/typesfast/status/2032469361748046024#m

Michigan #1 now in COOPER after Duke's close call last night. These are not adjusted for injuries, although that will hurt Duke a little bit too when we add them for our tournament forecast.
natesilver.net/p/cooper-mens…

From a purely electoral standpoint (not that I don't have opinions about it from a non-electoral standpoint) Iran has to be one of the dumber moves in recent memory. Maybe the base case is that things get back to normal, but the risks for Trump are weighted to the downside.
natesilver.net/p/cooper-wome…

Our women's COOPER ratings just dropped! (They are co-named after Cooper Flagg and Cynthia Cooper, after all.) Way more dominance than in the men's game. NCAA tournament forecasts to follow after the brackets are announced.

You can check my Twitter feed for the prominent names I was fighting with about this over the weekend (a big VC and a TV host). But more importantly, people in the White House and the Defense Department seemed to be engaged in exactly the same sort of magical thinking.


Varad Mehta (@varadmehta)

"Fuck those people who said it would all be over in a few days."

So fuck a bunch of Twitter randos? That's a weird thing to say. Anyone know why Big Nate is crashing out so hard over this? Just seems

It's a war so I'm gonna be very nice and not dunk on people who 100% deserve it but honestly fuck those people who said it would all be over in a few days.


Joe Weisenthal (@TheStalwart)

Oil tanker crossings through the Strait of Hormuz





— https://nitter.net/TheStalwart/status/2032103844759384211#m
"You can just do things" is basically the same heuristic as "Everyone has a plan until they get punched in the face" except for people who haven't been punched in the face yet.
I think (?) the U.S. has made it thru but I still want to know what kind of hallucinogens the person who came up with these WBC tiebreakers was taking.

Obviously this stuff is hard to price. But the notion that it's *just* vibes is silly. It's *some* vibes mediated by objective facts on the ground amid the fog of war. Such as oil tankers (reportedly) being attacked. And the U.S. not really having a coherent gameplan.

Anyway, good newsletter today:

natesilver.net/p/will-iran-b…

While the dynamics between the GOP and Democrats aren't identical, one can identify some parallels to Gaza. Opinion among activists was a leading indicator for Democrats overall, though it took a year or two to really break through.

So far, polling on Iran doesn't show that much opposition among rank-and-file Republicans, even as people like Tucker Carlson and Joe Rogan denounce it. But as @emckowndawson points out, that may be missing the forest for the trees.
You can always find the latest COOPER numbers here: natesilver.net/p/cooper-mens…

Nerdy note: We've changed the text on the chart to clarify that our offensive/defensive projections are literally just projecting points scored and allowed per game. So that reflects both pace + efficiency, which is how the internal logic of the model works.
ICYMI, we launched our COOPER NCAA ratings last night. We'll be updating these every day. Also women's ratings coming tomorrow or Friday, I expect.
COOPER men's NCAA basketball ratings. Silver Bulletin's brand new power ratings for all 365 men's Division I NCAA basketball teams.
COOPER women's NCAA basketball ratings. Silver Bulletin's brand new power ratings for all 363 women's Division I NCAA basketball teams.
Who’s ahead on the generic congressional ballot?. Our constantly-updating tracker of polls for the most important indicator in the race for Congress.
How popular is Donald Trump?. Silver Bulletin approval ratings for President Trump — and all presidents since Truman.
How popular is Elon Musk? . Silver Bulletin favorability ratings for the world's richest man.
Will Iran break MAGA? (by Eli McKown-Dawson). So far, there’s not much erosion in Trump’s polling among Republicans. But elite opinion can trickle down to the party base.
Introducing COOPER: Silver Bulletin's NCAA basketball rating system
Gas prices are set to go vertical. It's not exactly a bold prediction to suggest this will be bad for Trump's political standing.
2028 Republican primary draft
Can Talarico win in November?. Plus, were prediction markets right or wrong about Texas? And did Talarico beat Crockett because he was more moderate?
War isn't what it once was. Past precedents are less useful than you'd think when predicting a war's political impact. But Iran still poses downside risks for Trump.
13 thoughts on Anthropic, OpenAI and the Department of War
Who’s the real favorite in the Texas Senate primary? (by Eli McKown-Dawson). Nonpartisan polls give Crockett the edge. So why are prediction markets so bullish on Talarico?

Could Republicans blow the Texas Senate race? (by Eli McKown-Dawson). The Democratic primary is contentious, but the winner might get a shot at the sort of candidate who cost the GOP the Senate in 2022.
Which 2028 Democrats have the best electoral track record?
